comment_text.sn6.6:1.3: This pair feature in the next two suttas as well. For Subrahmā, see note on SN 2.17:1.1. The name Suddhāvāsa suggests he was from the Pure Abodes. Perhaps both were, which would explain their devotion to the Buddha and the Dhamma. But the commentary is silent. | Independent divinities (paccekabrahmā) are without a retinue, although not, it seems, without a friend.
comment_text.sn6.6:2.5: The Pali is ambiguous, but I think this should be a question. Compare the similar syntax at AN 6.62:7.8.
comment_text.sn6.6:3.1: As so often, divinities in Buddhist texts try to distinguish themselves by power, not by wisdom or compassion.
comment_text.sn6.6:5.1: The haṁsa is a swan or goose. Though rarely seen in most of India today, the swan is a fitting image in such elevated contexts. | The “hundred” of the next line should be distributed to the numbers in this line, too, per the commentary.
comment_text.sn6.6:5.2: Byagghīnisā (“night-tiger”?) appears only here and in another list of birds at Ja 547:420.1 (variant byagghinasā), where the commentary glosses sena, a “raptor”. “Tigerhawk” is not a real name, but it sounds cool.
comment_text.sn6.6:5.3: Jalate (“shines”) here and below is reflexive indicative.

root_text.sn6.6:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā divāvihāragato hoti paṭisallīno.
root_text.sn6.6:1.3: Atha kho subrahmā ca paccekabrahmā suddhāvāso ca paccekabrahmā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā paccekaṁ dvārabāhaṁ upanissāya aṭṭhaṁsu. Atha kho subrahmā paccekabrahmā suddhāvāsaṁ paccekabrahmānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.6:1.6: Asuko ca brahmaloko iddho ceva phīto ca, brahmā ca tatra pamādavihāraṁ viharati.
root_text.sn6.6:1.7: Āyāma, mārisa, yena so brahmaloko tenupasaṅkamissāma; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ brahmānaṁ saṁvejeyyāmā”ti.
root_text.sn6.6:1.8: “Evaṁ, mārisā”ti kho suddhāvāso paccekabrahmā subrahmuno paccekabrahmuno paccassosi.
root_text.sn6.6:2.1: Atha kho subrahmā ca paccekabrahmā suddhāvāso ca paccekabrahmā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso …pe… evameva—bhagavato purato antarahitā tasmiṁ brahmaloke pāturahesuṁ.
root_text.sn6.6:2.2: Addasā kho so brahmā te brahmāno dūratova āgacchante. Disvāna te brahmāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.6:2.4: “Āgatā kho mayaṁ, mārisa, amha tassa bhagavato santikā arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
root_text.sn6.6:2.5: Gaccheyyāsi pana tvaṁ, mārisa, tassa bhagavato upaṭṭhānaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassā”ti?
root_text.sn6.6:3.1: Evaṁ vutto, kho so brahmā taṁ vacanaṁ anadhivāsento sahassakkhattuṁ attānaṁ abhinimminitvā subrahmānaṁ paccekabrahmānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.6:3.2: “passasi me no tvaṁ, mārisa, evarūpaṁ iddhānubhāvan”ti?
root_text.sn6.6:3.4: “So khvāhaṁ, mārisa, evaṁmahiddhiko evaṁmahānubhāvo kassa aññassa samaṇassa vā brāhmaṇassa vā upaṭṭhānaṁ gamissāmī”ti?
root_text.sn6.6:4.1: Atha kho subrahmā paccekabrahmā dvisahassakkhattuṁ attānaṁ abhinimminitvā taṁ brahmānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.6:4.2: “passasi me no tvaṁ, mārisa, evarūpaṁ iddhānubhāvan”ti?
root_text.sn6.6:4.4: “Tayā ca kho, mārisa, mayā ca sveva bhagavā mahiddhikataro ceva mahānubhāvataro ca.
root_text.sn6.6:4.5: Gaccheyyāsi tvaṁ, mārisa, tassa bhagavato upaṭṭhānaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassā”ti?
root_text.sn6.6:4.6: Atha kho so brahmā subrahmānaṁ paccekabrahmānaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn6.6:7.1: Atha kho subrahmā ca paccekabrahmā suddhāvāso ca paccekabrahmā taṁ brahmānaṁ saṁvejetvā tatthevantaradhāyiṁsu.
root_text.sn6.6:7.2: Agamāsi ca kho so brahmā aparena samayena bhagavato upaṭṭhānaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassāti.

translation_text.sn6.6:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha had gone into retreat for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.sn6.6:1.3: Then the independent divinities Subrahmā and Suddhāvāsa went to the Buddha and stationed themselves one by each door-post. But Subrahmā said to Suddhāvāsa,
translation_text.sn6.6:1.4: “Good fellow, it’s the wrong time to pay homage to the Buddha.
translation_text.sn6.6:1.5: He has gone into retreat for the day’s meditation.
translation_text.sn6.6:1.6: But such and such realm of divinity is successful and prosperous, while the divinity living there is negligent.
translation_text.sn6.6:1.7: Come, let’s go to that realm of divinity and inspire awe in that Divinity!”
translation_text.sn6.6:2.1: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, they vanished from in front of the Buddha and appeared in that realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn6.6:2.2: That divinity saw those divinities coming off in the distance and said to them,
translation_text.sn6.6:2.3: “Well now, good fellows, where have you come from?”
translation_text.sn6.6:2.4: “Good fellow, we’ve come from the presence of the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.sn6.6:2.5: Shouldn’t you go to attend on that Blessed One?”
translation_text.sn6.6:3.1: When they had spoken, that divinity refused to accept their advice. He multiplied himself a thousand times and said to Subrahmā,
translation_text.sn6.6:3.2: “Good fellow, can’t you see that I have such psychic power?”
translation_text.sn6.6:3.4: “Since I have such psychic power and might, what other ascetic or brahmin should I go to and attend upon?”
translation_text.sn6.6:4.1: Then Subrahmā multiplied himself two thousand times and said to that Divinity,
translation_text.sn6.6:4.2: “Good fellow, can’t you see that I have such psychic power?”
translation_text.sn6.6:4.4: “That Buddha has even more psychic power and might than you or me.
translation_text.sn6.6:4.5: Shouldn’t you go to attend on that Blessed One?”
translation_text.sn6.6:4.6: Then that divinity addressed Subrahmā in verse:
translation_text.sn6.6:5.1: “There are three hundreds of phoenixes, four of swans,
translation_text.sn6.6:5.2: and five of tigerhawks. To he who practiced absorption
translation_text.sn6.6:5.3: belongs this palace; it shines, Divinity,
translation_text.sn6.6:6.3: A clever person, seeing the strife in form,
translation_text.sn6.6:6.4: its chronic trembling, takes no pleasure in it.”
translation_text.sn6.6:7.1: Then after inspiring awe in that divinity, the independent divinities Subrahmā and Suddhāvāsa vanished right there.
translation_text.sn6.6:7.2: And after some time that divinity went to attend on the Buddha.

variant_text.sn6.6:5.3: jalate ca → jalati ca (bj); jalateva (pts1ed, mr)

reference_text.sn6.6:1.1: bj13.266, cck15.198, csp1ed12.149, csp2ed12.149, ms12S1_1063, msdiv177, pts-vp-pli1ed1.147, pts-vp-pli2ed1.319
